meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve slow development speed (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome slow development speed. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kits: Ship Faster & Scale for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Stop manual coding. Use SaaS starter kits with Antigravity and Claude Code to eliminate slow development cycles and ship professional apps for clients faster.
content.answer_block: To eliminate slow development, leverage a pre-configured SaaS starter kit that includes authentication, database schema, and payment integration. By combining Antigravity's rapid component generation with Claude Code's autonomous CLI-based refactoring, you can transition from project kickoff to a deployable MVP in under 48 hours instead of weeks.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, the client is waiting, and you're still wrestling with boilerplate authentication logic. In the freelance world, slow development isn't just an annoyance; it’s a revenue-killing bottleneck that keeps you from scaling your business. You don't need another generic tutorial; you need a war-tested infrastructure that lets you move at the speed of thought.

Imagine offloading the heavy lifting of backend setup, type safety, and UI scaffolding to an automated pipeline. By integrating Antigravity for rapid architectural generation and Claude Code for intelligent, intent-based implementation, you transform your workflow from manual labor into a high-velocity assembly line. It’s time to stop reinventing the wheel and start shipping premium SaaS products.

steps.title: Architect the Foundation
steps.description: Define your SaaS domain model and authentication flow to ensure the starter kit aligns with client requirements immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust SaaS foundation using Next.js 15, Tailwind, and Supabase auth. Structure the repository for multi-tenancy and include a standard user profile schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'initialize project with next-auth and supabase schema'

steps.title: Scaffold UI Components
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate consistent, accessible UI components that maintain a professional aesthetic across the entire application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable dashboard layout including a sidebar, top-bar with profile dropdown, and a data-table component with server-side filtering.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate dashboard components based on shadcn-ui and tailwind'

steps.title: Implement Payment Logic
steps.description: Integrate Stripe billing cycles into your starter kit to ensure you can monetize the application from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Stripe webhook handler and subscription management API route that updates the user's 'subscription_status' in the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add stripe checkout session creation and webhook listener'

steps.title: Automate Type Safety
steps.description: Force type-safe interactions between the frontend and backend to minimize runtime errors and speed up debugging.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate TypeScript interfaces for all Supabase tables and create a Zod validation schema for the user onboarding form.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate zod schemas from existing database types'

steps.title: Continuous Deployment Workflow
steps.description: Configure your CI/CD pipeline to ensure that every push is tested and ready for production deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a GitHub Actions workflow that runs linting, type-checking, and unit tests on every pull request.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create github actions workflow for vercel deployment'

pro_tips: Always use a 'feature-first' folder structure to keep your codebase modular and readable for future client handoffs.
Inject your own 'Personal Component Library' into the starter kit to ensure your output looks unique, not like a generic template.
Use Claude Code's memory feature to store client-specific coding standards so you don't have to repeat instructions in every session.
Automate your database migrations using a dedicated migration script within your kit to avoid manual table tweaks during production.
Prioritize 'Server Actions' over standard API routes to keep your logic centralized and reduce latency in your SaaS app.
content.next_steps: After finishing this setup, document your custom starter kit as a private GitHub template. This allows you to spin up new projects in seconds, effectively turning your freelance work into a repeatable, high-margin product.
content.conversion_block: Stop wasting hours on boilerplate and start focusing on high-value features that close deals. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
